title: Capstone Case Study 1 - Phase 6 Cutover Planning & Production Go-Live

sections.title: Production Go-Live Weekend
sections.subtitle: The Most Critical Phase of the Project

sections.content: After months of implementation, configuration, SIT and UAT, ABC Electronics is ready to deploy SAP TM into Production. Every activity must be executed according to the approved Cutover Plan because any mistake could stop nationwide transportation operations.
During Saturday morning smoke testing, planners discover that outbound deliveries from the Pune plant are not generating Freight Units in Production, although everything worked correctly in QA.

sections.subject: URGENT - Go-Live Risk
sections.body: The Steering Committee meeting starts in 45 minutes. Please investigate whether this issue is caused by transport migration, master data, authorization or integration. We need a recommendation before the Go/No-Go meeting.

sections.title: Executive Decision
sections.question: Freight Units cannot be generated only for the Pune plant. Hyderabad, Bengaluru and Chennai are working correctly. What should the Steering Committee decide?

options.text: Evaluate business impact, determine whether Pune can operate temporarily using an approved contingency plan, and make a risk-based Go/No-Go decision.

sections.explanation: A Go/No-Go decision should be based on business impact, risk, available workarounds and executive approval-not solely on the existence of a defect.

sections.background: At 08:05 AM the first production Sales Order is created. Business users watch the system closely as the first Freight Unit, Transportation Proposal and Freight Order are generated.

sections.title: Senior Consultant Advice
sections.content: A successful Go-Live is not measured by whether zero issues occur. It is measured by whether critical business processes remain operational, risks are managed effectively and the business can continue operating with confidence.

sections.title: Real Project Experience
sections.content: Many SAP implementations experience minor issues during the first production weekend. Mature project teams rely on contingency plans, structured war-room communication and risk-based decision making rather than expecting a perfect deployment.

sections.title: Accenture Go-Live Scenario
sections.content: During the Go/No-Go meeting, a defect affects one manufacturing plant but the rest of the business is operational. Explain how you would assess business impact, available workarounds, project risk, executive approval and contingency planning before recommending whether to proceed with Go-Live.

sections.title: Phase 6 Complete
sections.content: Production deployment has been completed successfully. SAP TM is now live. The project enters the Hypercare phase, where consultants provide intensive production support and rapidly resolve business-critical incidents.
